item.title: Airfare Is Just the Beginning
item.description: Expensive plane tickets are a preview of what could come next.
item.dc:creator: Will Gottsegen

item.title: Today’sAtlanticTrivia: Chinese Science
item.description: Test your knowledge—and read our stories for a little extra help.
item.dc:creator: Drew Goins

item.title: Let a Book Annoy You
item.description: Sometimes, an angry note in the margin can be an expression of love.
item.dc:creator: Emma Sarappo

item.title: The Worst-Case Scenario for AI and the News Is Already Here
item.description: Audiovisual evidence is no match for a viral conspiracy theory that Benjamin Netanyahu is dead.
item.dc:creator: Yair Rosenberg

item.title: What Is Twitter’s Legacy, 20 Years Later?
item.description: An early Twitter exec reckons with the monster he helped create.
item.dc:creator: Charlie Warzel

item.title: A Day in Class With Plato, the Melania Trump–Mandated Robot Teacher
item.description: The first lady told us to imagine a future of humanoid instruction … so I did.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra Petri

item.title: The First Post-Reality Political Campaign
item.description: Hungary’s Viktor Orbán is waging cognitive warfare on a new scale.
item.dc:creator: Anne Applebaum

item.title: The Very Powerful Men Who Think Introspection Is Dumb
item.description: For some of America’s tech oligarchs, looking inward seems to be a waste of time better spent moving fast and breaking things.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Chatterton Williams

item.title: Cuba Doesn’t Care About Marxism
item.description: Washington and Havana used to fight over ideology. That’s not true anymore.
item.dc:creator: Quico Toro

item.title: Photos of the Week: Fallas Festival, Butterfly Gathering, Beach Violin
item.description: Traditional bull racing in Indonesia, puppies rescued from flooding in Hawaii, humanoid robots in China, and much more
item.dc:creator: Alan Taylor

item.title: Kristi Noem Is Gone. Now Mass Deportations Can Really Begin.
item.description: DHS has a new leader and a big pot of money to deliver on Trump’s campaign promise.
item.dc:creator: Nick Miroff

item.title: The Shocking Speed of China’s Scientific Rise
item.description: When will Chinese research pull ahead of the U.S.’s?
item.dc:creator: Ross Andersen

item.title: The Tension That Defines Modern Life
item.description: Most people have a smartphone. But many want to use it less.
item.dc:creator: Julie Beck

item.title: The Surprising Reason for the New Homophobia
item.description: Americans are burned-out, frustrated, and hunting for scapegoats.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Kornhaber

item.title: Building Tanks While the Ukrainians Master Drones
item.description: Ukrainian drones have made artillery and armored vehicles look obsolete. Why is the world still buying them?
item.dc:creator: Simon Shuster

item.title: A Radical Message for a Kids’ Movie
item.description: Hoppersoffers a surprising take on the typical talking-animal story.
item.dc:creator: David Sims

item.title: Shockingly, ICE Hasn’t Fixed the Airport Crisis
item.description: Perhaps because they're not trained to expedite the long lines
item.dc:creator: Alexandra Petri

item.title: Critics Have a New Way to Describe the Trump Administration
item.description: Calling his presidency a “regime” has some benefits, but it underestimates the resilience of the 250-year-old republic.
item.dc:creator: David A. Graham

item.title: Welcome to a Multidimensional Economic Disaster
item.description: The AI boom wasn’t built for the polycrisis.
item.dc:creator: Matteo Wong

item.title: A Turning Point in the Iran War
item.description: The president is discovering the high stakes of an escalation that damages energy facilities.
item.dc:creator: Shane Harris
